_gate.instruction: SkillPath has a project-specific cartoon neobrutalism contract from the current user brief and repo evidence. Preserve the 70/20/10 split: neobrutalist structure, cartoon personality, subtle motion.

project.name: SkillPath
project.context: Career OS for developers with onboarding, role-based roadmap, learning resources, quiz/project assessment, weekly sprint planning, jobs, and GitHub/project progress.
project.runtimeConstraint: Existing Next.js App Router and Supabase architecture must be preserved.
project.architectureConstraint: Additive UI system only; do not rewrite data, auth, API, or roadmap logic for visual changes.

designPhilosophy: SkillPath uses a cartoon career planner layer on top of existing neobrutalist product UI. The app should feel alive and memorable while staying readable, professional, and useful for developer learning workflows.

distinctiveMoves: Use cream paper workspace backgrounds with deterministic developer doodles per route.
Use the cat mentor as a compact companion for dashboard, roadmap, sprint, jobs, quiz, project, settings, onboarding, and auth surfaces.
Keep thick black borders, hard shadows, solid cards, and clear form hierarchy as the dominant structure.

sourceFreshnessPolicy.olderSourcePolicy: older sources may support durable principles only when labeled old-timeless; they cannot prove current modernity
sourceFreshnessPolicy.noLiveResearchFallback: set sourceFreshnessStatus to pending-live-verification and do not claim current-year modernity

internalVocabularyPolicy.rule: Use research vocabulary for internal audit only; translate it into product-native language before UI copy, public docs, or user-facing rationale unless the user asks for the research trace.

adaptiveUserConceptPolicy.overrideRequires: recorded product accessibility technical or evidence conflict

designFlexibilityPolicy.contractRole: Use docs/design-intent.json as review invariants and handoff structure, not as a frozen visual recipe unless the user explicitly locks a decision.
designFlexibilityPolicy.tokenLockingRule: Semantic roles are required, but exact primitive values stay flexible until repo evidence, accessibility validation, implementation constraints, or explicit user approval locks them.
designFlexibilityPolicy.signatureMovePolicy: Record the required experience outcome separately from candidate implementation moves; replace a candidate move when another move better fits the product.
designFlexibilityPolicy.libraryVisualLanguagePolicy: Libraries supply behavior, accessibility, primitives, and delivery speed; they must not dictate final composition, theme, morphology, or visual language.
designFlexibilityPolicy.literalAnchorPolicy: Translate anchors into workflow, hierarchy, density, typography, material behavior, state language, and interaction grammar before requiring literal props, marks, or chrome.
designFlexibilityPolicy.spatialMetaphorPolicy: Do not default anchors to room, darkroom, counting room, control room, war room, studio, lab, cockpit, or command center. Use place metaphors only when the product truly depends on a physical place model.
designFlexibilityPolicy.externalInspirationPolicy: External websites and examples are candidate evidence for constraints, mechanics, and quality bars; do not copy their layout rhythm, palette, component skin, brand posture, or visual metaphor.

literalTranslationPolicy.allowedLiteralUse: Only use literal anchor artifacts when they serve a named product function, control, state, or task overlay.
literalTranslationPolicy.forbiddenLiteralUse: Do not turn anchor artifacts into decorative wallpaper, required chrome, default texture, or unavoidable theme props.

spatialAutopilotPolicy.allowedOnlyWhen: The product has a real physical place model, operational environment, or user workflow that depends on that place metaphor.
spatialAutopilotPolicy.replacementPreference: Use artifacts, custody flows, instruments, data behaviors, material systems, editorial systems, service rituals, or interaction mechanisms.
spatialAutopilotPolicy.reviewQuestion: Could this anchor still work if the word "room" was removed? If not, revise before UI code.

categoryCodes.specificityRule: A category code is only valid if a reader unfamiliar with the project can visualize a specific aesthetic direction from the text alone, without seeing the UI and without knowing the product name.
categoryCodes.antiLeakageRule: Listing a cliche identifies a trap; it does not endorse an aesthetic. Examples in the brief are NOT target aesthetics for any project. Each category code must carry an explicit rejection note so the cliche cannot quietly become the target.
failingExamples: clean typography (too abstract, applies to anything)
passingExamples: children's storybook illustration site: hand-painted gouache textures with irregular hand-lettered titles, off-grid spreads with whitespace gutters, page-turn pacing rather than scroll (instantly recognizable as kids book category default)

luxury car configurator: full-bleed monochrome photography on black, ultra-thin sans-serif tracked wide, slow horizontal scroll with locked vertical alignment, micro-counters that tick instead of slide (instantly recognizable as luxury auto category default)

academic philosophy journal: high-contrast black-on-cream, book-class serif body at 11pt with generous leading, footnote markers with hover panels, numbered table-of-contents navigation, no hero imagery (instantly recognizable as academic journal category default)
categoryCodes.passingExamplesPolicy: These examples illustrate the description format only. They are AI-defaultable cliches of their categories and must NOT be adopted as target aesthetics for any project.
commonAiSafeClichesToReject: dev-tool default: condensed tabular numerics with minimal chrome and monospace code blocks on dark slate background, sans-serif metadata at 11-12px, monochrome status dots, single-line settings rows
AI-startup landing default: purple-to-pink gradient hero with floating 3D glass cards, sans-serif display type at 700-900 weight, vague hero copy, three-up feature grid below the fold
health/wellness app default: mint accent on white surface with coral status indicators, rounded pill-shaped buttons, friendly sans-serif at high weight, soft drop shadows on cards
SaaS admin default: left-side icon-only nav, top utility bar, three-card KPI row above a single data table, neutral grey-on-white with one accent color, modal-driven detail flows
marketing site default: hero image with one-line headline plus subhead, three feature tiles below, two pricing tiers, testimonial carousel, footer link grid
categoryCodes.commonAiSafeClichesPolicy: If the project sits anywhere near one of these AI-safe defaults, the matching cliche must appear in candidateEntries with an explicit rejection note. Software products pattern-match one of these without intervention; naming the trap is required even when the trap is uncomfortable to admit.
categoryCodes.selfTestRule: Read each category code aloud to someone unfamiliar with the project. If they cannot visualize a specific aesthetic direction from the text alone, the code is too abstract; revise. If they say "that is basically the X cliche", the description is specific enough; the cliche then belongs on the reject list with a rejection note, not as a candidate target.

morphologicalExploration.uncomfortableCombinationRule: Highlight at least one combination that feels instinctively wrong or uncomfortable but can be argued with product logic. The uncomfortable combination proves the matrix actually spans the design space; if every combination feels safe, shippable, and unobjectionable, the matrix is clustering in the safe-creative zone.

renameTest.testCategoryDistanceRule: The three test categories must be remote from each other and from the actual product (for example, if the product is a health app, test against fintech dashboard, kids educational game, and industrial equipment monitoring console).
renameTest.testCategoryFreshnessRule: Pick fresh test categories per anchor. Reusing the same triple across every anchor lets the agent memorize the pass condition instead of stress-testing the anchor. The illustrative triple in testCategoryDistanceRule is not a fixed test set.

verdictScoring.STRONG PASS: UI still coherent in 0 of 3 renamed categories.
verdictScoring.PASS: UI still coherent in 1 of 3. Pass with note.
verdictScoring.REVISE: UI still coherent in 2 of 3. Anchor is too generic. Revise the anchor to add product-specific constraints until it fails in at least 2 of 3.
verdictScoring.DISCARD: UI still coherent in 3 of 3. Anchor is category-agnostic. Discard immediately.

anchorCandidates.categoryCodeOverlapRule: List any Section 3 category codes this candidate accidentally inherits, with reasoning.

metadata.freshnessRule: Research dossier is stale when researchVerifiedAt is null or older than freshnessWindowDays. Stale dossiers must run research-design.md before UI implementation. User-explicit redesign requests bypass freshness and force fresh research regardless of age.

sourceTypePolicy.official: required for implementation and package/API claims when available
sourceTypePolicy.industry: allowed for trend discovery after fit filtering
sourceTypePolicy.opinion: advisory only; cannot lock design decisions alone
sourceTypePolicy.oldTimeless: allowed for durable principles only, not current-year modern claims
sourceTypePolicy.userProvided: candidate evidence and concept constraint, not automatic final prescription

derivedTokenLogic.anchorReference: Developer career planner with bright sticky tabs, roadmap checkpoints, project stickers, and stamped progress states.
derivedTokenLogic.colorDerivationSource: Retain existing SkillPath primitives: cream paper base, black ink borders, yellow priority/action, blue technology/job surfaces, pink roadmap energy, green completion, orange project/deploy energy, purple quiz/settings accents.
derivedTokenLogic.spacingDerivationSource: Planner surfaces use generous card padding and predictable gaps. Background doodles remain fixed and low opacity; they do not affect layout or reading density.
derivedTokenLogic.typographyDerivationSource: Display headings stay on Space Grotesk for bold sticker-like labels. Body and operational copy stay on Inter for readability in forms, filters, resources, and review feedback.
derivedTokenLogic.motionDerivationSource: Use slow floating doodles, pressed button/card feedback, and mascot idle motion. Disable all decorative movement through prefers-reduced-motion.
derivedTokenLogic.colorSpace: Prefer OKLCH for newly generated CSS tokens when supported; preserve existing design-system token formats and document fallback color space.
derivedTokenLogic.spatialBaseUnit: Name the base spacing unit, major multiples, density exceptions, and optical exceptions before writing spacing values.
derivedTokenLogic.typeScaleMethod: Prefer fluid clamp() type scales when supported; name ratio, role contrast, balance/wrap behavior, and numeric typography needs.
derivedTokenLogic.motionBudget: Name micro, layout, entrance, easing, stagger, and reduced-motion budgets; prefer transform/opacity for high-frequency motion.
derivedTokenLogic.validationRule: Every semantic token role must trace to anchorReference; keep exact primitive values flexible unless locked by repo evidence, accessibility validation, implementation constraints, or explicit user approval.

tokenContinuityClassification.rule: For each token category, classify whether the choice is anchor-derived (causally tied to anchorReference real-world reality), continuity-retained (kept from a previous design iteration without re-derivation), or newly-introduced (fresh choice not tied to anchor). Continuity-retained is acceptable; pretending continuity is derivation is not.

motionPaletteDecision.productCategorySignal: Cartoon Career OS for Developers plus Neo-brutalist Learning Game.
motionPaletteDecision.densityDecisionSource: Use low decorative motion because SkillPath is a productivity and learning app with text-heavy forms, cards, roadmaps, jobs, and submissions.
motionPaletteDecision.spatialDecision: No new 3D/canvas layer for this pass. Existing CSS/SVG/lucide/framer-motion assets provide the required personality with lower hydration and performance risk.

aiSafeUiAudit.failureDefinition: AI-safe UI uses template cards, generic marks, decorative grid or line wallpaper, calibration-mark wallpaper, test/demo/placeholder copy, terminal-only user paths, safe palettes, glow backgrounds, or copied scaffold composition.
aiSafeUiAudit.interchangeabilityTest: If this UI can be renamed from project gabut to another product category without changing composition, palette, iconography, and motion, revise it.
requiredProductSpecificSignals: Route-specific doodles map to real SkillPath workflows: roadmap maps, sprint notes, job radar, quiz question marks, project deploy stickers, settings tools.
Motion is limited to slow background float, mascot idle movement, and pressed button/card feedback.
The mascot accessory system gives each learning/productivity area a recognizable companion state.
aiSafeUiAudit.paletteExplorationRule: Use a visually exploratory product-derived palette with WCAG contrast and status clarity.
aiSafeUiAudit.backgroundPatternRule: Lines, grids, scanlines, noise, glows, blobs, logos, calibration marks, and geometry must serve a named product function; never use grid, line, or calibration-mark backgrounds as first-output filler. Measurement and inspection marks belong to task overlays or controls, not page wallpaper.

aiColorAudit.failureDefinition: AI color drift uses safe defaults before deriving roles from the product anchor.
aiColorAudit.reviewQuestion: Why does this palette belong to this product?

motionSpatialCourageAudit.defaultStance: Treat motion, scroll choreography, canvas, WebGL, and 3D as first-class options.
motionSpatialCourageAudit.rejectionRule: State a product reason and replacement interaction quality before omitting 3D/canvas. Package count or vague performance fear is not enough.
motionSpatialCourageAudit.reviewQuestion: Is the interaction as expressive as the product can responsibly support?

aiSafeUiAudit.reviewQuestion: Does each route show a SkillPath-specific learning, career, job, quiz, project, or profile signal without hiding content?

productionContentPolicy.userFacingCopyRule: Visible UI copy must be product-ready and task-specific. Do not ship testing, demo, sample, placeholder, lorem, TODO, coming soon, or scaffold labels unless they are real product states.
productionContentPolicy.terminalDependencyRule: User-facing workflows must be operable through the UI unless the product is explicitly a CLI, developer tool, or operational runbook. Terminal commands belong in setup and deployment docs, not as the only path for core user tasks.
productionContentPolicy.reviewQuestion: Can this UI be shipped to real users without removing test/demo copy or terminal-only workflow dependencies?

libraryDecisions.purpose: Reuse existing styling, icon, and micro-interaction dependencies already present in the repository. Do not add a new animation or illustration package for this pass.
libraryDecisions.fallbackIfUnavailable: Use static SVG/CSS shapes and existing brutal components.
libraryDecisions.selectionPolicy: Existing dependencies are sufficient for subtle transform-only motion, stickers, icons, and mascot scenes.
libraryDecisions.officialScaffolderPolicy: Not applicable; this is an additive UI pass in an existing Next.js project.
libraryDecisions.frameworkNeutralityPolicy: Existing Next.js App Router architecture remains the source of truth.

tokenLockingPolicy.promotionRule: Promote flexible tokens to locked only when user approval, repo evidence, accessibility validation, or implementation constraints require it.

colorTruth.intent: Choose semantic palette roles from the product context "Project brief unresolved. The AI agent must ask for or infer product context before implementation.", repo evidence, and accessibility. Reject scaffold or SaaS palette defaults.

mutationRules.mobile: Reorder, merge, or disclose content for mobile. Reject scale-only shrink.
mutationRules.tablet: Regroup for tablet instead of width-only desktop reduction.
mutationRules.desktop: Use space intentionally; avoid equal-weight modules without evidence.

motionSystem.purpose: Use expressive motion when it improves hierarchy, continuity, feedback, memorability, or confidence. Verify new motion libraries with official docs.

viewportBehavior.mobile: Recompose for touch, task priority, and constrained attention.
viewportBehavior.tablet: Regroup surfaces for medium width without cloning desktop or mobile.
viewportBehavior.desktop: Use space for hierarchy and scanability; avoid template grids.

designExecutionHandoff.primaryExperienceGoal: Define the main fullstack product journey for project gabut from repo evidence, brief, and docs.

surfacePlan.goal: Choose the first task path from product evidence; reject template shells.

mobile.rationale: Reorder, merge, or disclose content for mobile. Reject scale-only shrink.

tablet.rationale: Regroup for tablet instead of width-only desktop reduction.

desktop.rationale: Use space intentionally; avoid equal-weight modules without evidence.

interactionStateMatrix.notes: Refine states from project language and anchor; reject anonymous panels.

expressionFlexibility.lockingRule: A candidate move becomes required only after repo evidence, product function, accessibility need, or explicit user approval makes it necessary.

taskFlowNarrative: Entry: start project gabut from real evidence, not a generic opener.
Resolution: define proof, feedback, recovery, and next action.
designExecutionHandoff.signatureMoveRationale: Choose one project-specific visual, motion, type, or interaction move.

creativeCommitment.failureMode: generic quality words without a real-world reference fail

dimensions.question: Is the UI authored and product-specific?
Does the UI follow contract, priorities, and accessibility?
Do type, spacing, color, and states form one system?
Does the UI preserve clarity, feedback, and confidence?
Is motion purposeful, performant, reduced-motion-safe, and on-tone?

contextHygiene.approvedExternalConstraintUsage: Convert approved external constraints into current-project rules; do not imitate source surfaces.
contextHygiene.externalWebsiteReferencePolicy: Use outside websites for mechanics, constraints, and quality bar analysis only. Do not copy layout rhythm, palette, component skin, visual metaphor, or brand posture.

requiredDesignSections: Spacing, Layout Rhythm, and Density Strategy
Responsive Strategy and Cross-Viewport Adaptation Matrix
Component Language, Morphology, and Shared Patterns
Implementation Notes for Future UI Tasks
